sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel data documented for Diablo (1997). Point-and-click movement system with isometric perspective.

special_mechanics.name: Point-and-click Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player controls character movement via mouse clicks rather than direct input. Character navigates procedurally generated dungeons with isometric perspective.

special_mechanics.name: Dungeon Navigation
special_mechanics.description: Procedurally generated levels with varying layouts (catacombs with corridors, caves with non-linear paths). No documented platforming or physics-based movement.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Diablo is primarily a dungeon-crawling action RPG with point-and-click movement controlled via mouse. No platform physics, gravity, jump mechanics, or sub-pixel rendering documented. Movement parameters are not applicable to this game's design paradigm. Character movement is contextual interaction-based rather than physics-driven.
